chapter1.start.t001: At night, the school building felt far larger than it did during the day.
chapter1.start.t002: The corridor ahead was dark, and only my footsteps echoed strangely loud.
chapter1.start.t003: There should not have been anyone there, yet I felt as if I was searching for someone.
chapter1.start.t004: I tried to call his name, but my throat wouldn't move.
chapter1.start.t005: Because I knew that even if I called out, it was already too late.
chapter1.start.t008: Instead, I thought I heard a windowpane rattle somewhere far away.
chapter1.start.t009: Maybe it was the wind. Maybe I imagined it.
chapter1.start.t010: But that day, too, something must have sounded just like that.
chapter1.start.t015: and that phrase I had heard too many times: "I'm fine."
chapter1.start.t018: A dark room. The ceiling. Shallow breaths.
chapter1.start.t019: Only the color of the streetlight slipping through the curtains told me the night wasn't over yet.
chapter1.start.t021: Dreams turn vague the moment you wake.
chapter1.start.t022: But the feeling left deep in my chest was almost always the same.
chapter1.start.t023: The feeling that I was late.
chapter1.start.t024: The feeling that I would never make it in time.
chapter1.start.t028: My head was too clear to go back to sleep.
chapter1.start.t029: My eyes fell on the running shoes still sitting beside my bed.
chapter1.start.t030: It had been a long time since I wore them.
chapter1.start.t031: Even so, I couldn't throw them away. It felt less like attachment and more like punishment.
chapter1.start.t033: Not because it let me stop thinking.
chapter1.start.t034: Because while I ran, I could believe I wasn't falling behind.
chapter1.start.t035: But then I hurt my ankle, and that ended too.
chapter1.start.t036: Once a feeling collapses, it doesn't come back as easily as you expect.
chapter1.start.t037: It had been the same when my father collapsed.
chapter1.start.t038: Everyone thought he was only busy with work.
chapter1.start.t039: He did, the people around him did, and probably I did too.
chapter1.start.t041: The things dismissed by that one sentence
chapter1.start.t044: The first commute of the new term.
chapter1.start.t045: The residential streets still carried the sleepy air of early morning.
chapter1.start.t046: A bicycle chain. A morning show drifting from someone's house.
chapter1.start.t047: Only the sound of cars in the distance told me the world was moving again today.
chapter1.start.t048: I didn't change my route because it was too much trouble.
chapter1.start.t049: A new class. A new seat.
chapter1.start.t050: On a day when too many things were changing, I had no energy left to change the road too.
chapter1.start.t051: Just before the intersection, the light turned yellow.
chapter1.start.t052: If I ran, I could make it.
chapter1.start.t053: Once I crossed the rotary, it wouldn't even take a minute.
chapter1.start.t056: I couldn't remember the feeling of stepping forward.
chapter1.start.t057: That feeling. The lack of hesitation when kicking off the ground with all my strength.
chapter1.start.t058: Even now that my ankle had healed, that alone hadn't returned.
chapter1.start.t061: It wasn't like I had been in a hurry anyway.
chapter1.start.t062: Telling myself that, I took another step.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t002: The air of a new school term still felt a little false.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t003: A new class, a new seat, new relationships.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t004: All of them looked clean and bright at first.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t005: Maybe everyone was simply trying to make them look that way.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t006: The classroom was full of morning noise.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t007: Laughter. Chairs scraping. Someone calling a name.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t008: Surrounded by sounds like that, I felt as if only my outline had grown thin.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t009: I placed my bag at my new seat.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t010: It wasn't by the window, but not too close to the blackboard either.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t011: For better or worse, it was a place that wouldn't stand out.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t012: Oh, good morning! You're Kirishima, right? My seat's next to yours?
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t014: Thank goodness. I was worried you might be the super silent type.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t015: I mean, you still kind of look like the super silent type!
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t016: Is that something you say the first time you meet someone?
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t017: Of course. It's true, isn't it?
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t018: I'm Sakura Sakurazaki. Nice to meet you, Kirishima.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t019: Yu Kirishima. Nice to meet you.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t021: Then from today on, you're Yu!
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t022: You just decide that on your own, huh.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t025: People like her usually pulled the air around them along at their own speed.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t026: I wouldn't say I disliked it.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t027: It was just a little dazzling.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t028: Hey, hey, did you already take a picture of the class list?
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t029: I heard our homeroom teacher is really strict.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t030: But maybe that means they'll get super serious about the school festival!
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t032: That's exactly why we need to build up energy now!
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t033: A new school term only happens once, you know.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t034: She smiled as she said it.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t035: Straight ahead, without a trace of doubt.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t036: That smile suited the brightness of the classroom almost perfectly.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t039: Whoa, it's starting. Let's talk later!
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t041: Even the way she switched gears was quick.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t042: People like that usually knew how to live inside a group.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t043: That didn't mean people who managed well never got hurt.
chapter1.chapter1_morning_gate.t044: ...Thinking that way probably said something ugly about me.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t001: The teacher introduced himself, checked textbooks, and gave announcements no one cared about.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t002: First-day homeroom was usually like that.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t003: But inside that "usually," there was one small disturbance.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t005: Teacher: All right, everyone, settle down.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t006: Teacher: Starting today, a transfer student will be joining this class. Be friendly.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t007: In an instant, the direction of the room changed.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t009: Attention gathers around anything that stands out, even without a reason.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t014: Her voice wasn't exactly quiet. It simply didn't spread farther than necessary.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t015: Teacher: That's it? Any hobbies? Things you like?
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t017: Teacher: I see. Then your seat is by the window in the back.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t019: For some reason, only that name stayed strangely in my ears.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t020: She sat at her desk and looked out the window.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t021: It seemed less like looking and more like confirming something.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t022: I didn't know what she saw.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t023: The sky, the schoolyard, or something else entirely.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t024: Even after she sat down, the classroom noise didn't fully return.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t025: Everyone was interested, but everyone was measuring how to speak to her.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t026: I knew that kind of distance well.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t027: I was probably on the same side of it.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t029: But she seems kind of hard to approach.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t034: Maybe she thinks you're hard to approach too, Yu.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t038: Every one of her reactions was light.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t039: Because of that, the person watching could answer lightly too.
chapter1.chapter1_homeroom.t041: Probably for the people around them, and for themselves too.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t001: The short break between first and second period.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t002: The air of the classroom thinned a little.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t003: I didn't dislike gaps like this.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t004: No one was talking about anything too deeply, so I didn't have to hear unnecessary words.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t005: I stepped into the hallway to buy a drink.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t006: Just as I turned the corner, I heard a small voice.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t008: My feet stopped before I could think.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t009: The girl at the end of the hallway was someone I recognized.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t010: Recognized wasn't quite the word. She was a face I couldn't have forgotten.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t014: But when you say it like that, it sounds a little distant.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t015: We haven't talked since elementary school.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t018: But you haven't changed much, Yu.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t023: It looked as if she almost added that, but Mahiru swallowed the words.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t024: You surprised me. I didn't expect you to be here.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t025: Ahaha. I didn't transfer in or anything.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t026: I came back a little while ago.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t027: I just never found the right timing to see you.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t028: Mahiru had always been someone who smiled softly.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t030: But the fact that it hadn't changed made it look a little unnatural.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t032: Next time, I want to talk a little more properly.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t035: With only that, Mahiru walked on ahead.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t036: All that remained in the hallway was the afterimage of her footsteps.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t038: The blank years between us were too long for that word to fit neatly.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t039: But she wasn't someone I could make into a complete stranger either.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t040: That distance was the hardest kind to deal with.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t042: That was what it felt like she had said.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t044: It only sounded that way to me.
chapter1.chapter1_breaktime.t045: I was probably the strange one.
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t002: The classroom felt a little warmer than it had that morning.
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t003: Some students had already formed groups, while others stood just outside them.
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t004: Human relationships always took shape faster than I expected.
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t005: Yuuuun, what are you doing for lunch?
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t006: If you're alone, eat with me!
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t007: I don't have a reason to refuse.
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t009: But if you're going to the school store, tell me first. I don't join the yakisoba bread wars.
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t010: Do not say it like a faction.
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t011: It is! That place is basically a battlefield!
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t012: When Sakura talked, the initiative rarely came back to the other person.
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t013: But it wasn't unpleasant, probably because she was actually watching the other person's reactions.
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t014: Yu, have you been at this school since before?
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t015: I feel like I've seen you, but also like I haven't.
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t017: Then I just didn't remember. Sorry, sorry.
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t019: That's not something you need to apologize for.
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t020: I actually worry about that kind of thing a lot.
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t022: Well, I remember now, so problem solved!
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t023: For some reason, those words caught on me.
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t025: I choose the way that is easiest to live!
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t026: She smiled in her usual way.
chapter1.chapter1_lunch.t030: Someone who could say that was probably strong.
chapter1.chapter1_after_school_intro.t00: When people left the classroom, the room revealed a width I hadn't noticed during the day.
Even the rows of desks and chairs looked a little unfamiliar.
I had nothing in particular planned today.
No club, no committee duty, no one calling me anywhere.
All I had to do was go straight home.
And yet I couldn't bring myself to stand up right away.
chapter1.chapter1_after_school_intro.t01: Sakura laughed and began straightening the handouts.
Her movements had no wasted motion.
Her hands looked used to doing what someone had asked of her, as if that were natural.
chapter1.chapter1_after_school_intro.t02: The expression vanished from Sakura's face.
As if the light had gone out first,
chapter1.chapter1_after_school_intro.t03: Is there something on my face?
I am kind of sleepy today. The new term is weirdly tiring, right?
Then I am heading out. See you tomorrow!
As if what I had seen a moment earlier had never happened.
chapter1.chapter1_after_school_intro.t04: Maybe I was the strange one for looking for meaning in an expression that lasted only an instant.
didn't look like a smile had peeled away.
It looked like a place where nothing had been put in the first place.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t001: I left the classroom to go home.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t002: The school building at dusk had a thinness of sound different from the daytime.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t003: Voices and club activity shouts sounded distant, as if a wall stood between us.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t004: When I passed the library, I saw a figure through the door that had been left slightly open.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t006: The evening color had soaked into even the collar of her uniform, making her look quieter than she had at noon.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t007: She stood in front of a shelf, a book in her hand.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t009: It was such a small motion, but for some reason it stayed with me.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t010: On the desk sat a small music player and headphones.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t011: Maybe she had been listening to something until a moment ago.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t013: She seemed to be looking at the staff lines drawn on the book's cover.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t015: Somewhere far away, I thought I heard a single piano note.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t016: I didn't know whether it had really sounded, or whether I only thought it had.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t019: For a brief instant, she looked surprised.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t023: With only that, she quietly picked up her headphones and disappeared deeper into the library.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t024: That probably hadn't been a moment to call out to her.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t026: But another part of me wondered whether something might have changed if I had.
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t028: What kind of feeling did Kotoha have when she reached for that book?
chapter1.chapter1_library_pass.t030: Thinking that much on the first day was probably self-important.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t001: On my way to the shoe lockers, I could see the sky through the window on the stair landing.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t002: The last of the sunset was slowly thinning.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t003: The end of a day always came faster than I expected.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t004: For an instant, I thought I saw someone's shadow reflected by the window.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t008: If we meet twice in one day, does that not feel a little like fate?
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t013: When I followed her gaze, a thin cloud was drifting at the edge of the sky.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t014: Today's sky has a slightly lonely color.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t015: Can you really tell that much from color?
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t017: On days when the sky is lonely, people tend to be quiet.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t018: What is that supposed to mean?
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t020: She smiled when she said it.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t021: But only her eyes seemed a little far away.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t022: Did you always say things like that?
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t023: No. I think I say them more now.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t024: That way, I don't lose sight of things.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t027: For some reason, that answer felt a little frightening.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t029: Be careful on your way home.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t031: So I don't lose sight of things.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t032: It was the second phrase today that had caught on me.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t033: From the very first day, something felt slightly out of alignment.
chapter1.chapter1_twilight_stairs.t034: That feeling alone remained, without turning properly into words.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t001: In the end, by the time I realized I had forgotten something and returned to the classroom, the building was almost night.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t004: Outside the window, only the lights of town blurred faintly.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t005: The place that had been so full of voices that morning
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t006: looked like a different building at night.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t007: I picked up the notebook I had left at my desk.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t008: That was all I needed to do.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t009: And yet I couldn't bring myself to leave right away.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t010: Sakura's face, gone blank for only an instant.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t011: Kotoha's fingers, stopped on the book cover.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t012: Mahiru's words about not losing sight of things.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t013: None of them had a clear meaning.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t014: I told myself I was thinking too much for impressions from the first day.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t016: But in me, the memory of being too late because I failed to notice
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t017: weighed far more than the memory of noticing too much and being wrong.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t020: I walked to the window and looked up at the night sky.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t023: I felt as if it had been a night like this.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t025: Shin had seemed the same as always.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t026: At least, he had looked that way to me.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t027: We talked about stupid things, laughed,
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t028: and when we parted, he said, "See you tomorrow."
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t031: Maybe the voice asking for help had been there long before.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t032: But I had picked up nothing.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t033: It was the same with my father.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t035: I always think I am watching,
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t039: The moment I said it aloud, I hated the word.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t042: The person who says it can feel relieved,
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t043: while the person it is said to grows silent.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t044: I had seen that happen too many times.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t045: That was why I hated that word.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t046: Even so, people used it so easily.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t049: And probably many times from now on.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t051: I would probably get caught on it.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t052: Looking up at a sky with no stars, I thought.
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t055: Will I be able to reach toward
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t056: whatever is hidden behind that voice?
chapter1.chapter1_last_classroom.t062: the only thing I can do now
chapter2.chapter2_start.t001: By the time April had passed its midpoint,
chapter2.chapter2_start.t002: the cherry blossoms had begun to fall little by little, and pale green was starting to mix into the trees.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t003: The schoolyard, once outlined in soft pink, was growing closer to the colors of summer with each passing day.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t004: Changes like that usually move along before you notice them.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t005: Human relationships are probably the same way.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t006: The mood in class had settled down far more than it had at the start of April.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t008: but the usual seats and the usual faces were starting to take shape.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t009: Groups that called each other by first name,
chapter2.chapter2_start.t010: girls laughing over the same topic every morning,
chapter2.chapter2_start.t011: boys who shoved earphones into their ears first thing and stayed silent.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t012: Those already-decided arrangements were slowly putting down roots in the classroom.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t013: As for my place... I still had no idea.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t019: You should be like, "The morning's here! Yay!"
chapter2.chapter2_start.t022: ...Sakurazaki, are you like this every morning?
chapter2.chapter2_start.t026: I'm pretty sure she said that with a straight face.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t028: What part of that was a compliment?
chapter2.chapter2_start.t029: That she has a great deal of energy.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t030: Kotoha, you can be kind of airheaded sometimes!
chapter2.chapter2_start.t031: Hanazono tilted her head a little.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t032: She didn't seem aware of it herself.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t033: ...No, for a moment I wondered if this was calculated,
chapter2.chapter2_start.t037: A bright smile that suited the classroom perfectly.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t038: Spring sunlight came in through the window and lit her expression just right.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t039: Kotoha, you'd be even cuter if you smiled more.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t043: Kotoha's brows moved ever so slightly.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t044: It was less a smile and more a troubled look,
chapter2.chapter2_start.t045: but it wasn't bad in its own way.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t047: What? I'm not! I'm complimenting her!
chapter2.chapter2_start.t052: Kotoha's lips curved upward by the smallest amount.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t054: Exchanges like that had already become natural to us.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t055: During the first week of April, this class had been, to me,
chapter2.chapter2_start.t056: nothing more than a collection of strangers whose faces and names I could barely remember.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t057: Before I knew it, I could laugh over pointless morning talk like this.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t059: Just sharing the same space every day is enough for a place to slowly form around you.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t060: ...That smile stopped for just an instant.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t061: When Sakura laughed and let her gaze drift out the window,
chapter2.chapter2_start.t062: her expression vanished for the briefest sliver of time.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t063: She wasn't angry. She wasn't depressed.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t064: Her face simply held nothing at all.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t069: Oh, did you not sleep enough?
chapter2.chapter2_start.t071: You've got little bags under your eyes.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t079: This time it was the same smile as before.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t081: It had lasted less than a second,
chapter2.chapter2_start.t082: so it was probably more likely that my eyes had played a trick on me.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t084: I didn't want to hide behind the words "just my imagination."
chapter2.chapter2_start.t085: Oh, right! How about the rooftop at lunch today?
chapter2.chapter2_start.t086: Let's invite Mahiru too and go with the four of us!
chapter2.chapter2_start.t088: They started opening it last year!
chapter2.chapter2_start.t089: I asked the class duty student!
chapter2.chapter2_start.t092: Before I could ask anything else, Sakura went off to talk to the girl in the seat in front of her.
chapter2.chapter2_start.t095: Inside the morning school building, ordinary time flowed on as usual.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t003: The sound of textbook pages turning overlapped with the teacher's voice.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t004: Outside the window, PE had started on the schoolyard.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t005: Another class was moving around in a hazy, half-focused way.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t006: Inside the classroom, everything was the same as usual.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t007: Sakura sat in the seat in front of me.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t008: She reacted to the teacher often, speaking up without hesitation.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t010: More than anyone else in class, she could shift the mood of the room.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t011: When someone like that is around, a class runs smoothly.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t012: Everyone finds it a little easier to speak.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t013: Everyone starts to understand when it's okay to laugh.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t014: That was why Sakura's place in the class had already been decided.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t015: "The cheerful girl." "The girl who's easy to talk to."
chapter2.chapter2_class.t016: Everyone had naturally attached labels like that to her.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t017: The teacher made a joke that was actually a little funny.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t019: At that moment, Sakura laughed louder than anyone.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t020: Loud enough for the whole classroom to hear.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t022: Everyone looked like they were having fun.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t024: Her laughter carried on just a little too long, stopping a beat after everyone else's had begun to fade,
chapter2.chapter2_class.t025: and the instant Sakura's voice fell silent...
chapter2.chapter2_class.t028: Truly no longer than a single blink.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t030: Not the girl beside her, not the one in front, not the teacher.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t031: What remained after her laughter disappeared
chapter2.chapter2_class.t033: As if the light had gone out first,
chapter2.chapter2_class.t035: The next moment, Sakura had already started taking notes.
chapter2.chapter2_class.t037: As if making sure no one would notice.

chapter6.chapter6_start.t001: August. It was the middle of summer break.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t002: The closing ceremony had ended, and July had become August.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t003: Nearly two weeks had passed since that day at the pool.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t004: Ever since the rainy season ended, it felt as if the world's temperature had become something else entirely.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t005: The asphalt stored the light and returned heat from beneath my feet.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t006: The cicadas never stopped. Their voices felt like the only proof that time was moving.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t007: When I stayed in my room, it felt like I was standing still.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t008: July had become August, but inside me, something remained suspended and unmoving.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t009: If I closed the curtains, I could block out some of the brightness outside.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t010: Only the sound couldn't be blocked.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t011: No matter how thick the curtains were, the cicadas told me it was summer.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t012: Once the second term began, preparations for the school festival would start.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t013: I could feel that kind of talk approaching from far away.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t014: The school website had already posted a notice for the special school festival issue.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t015: I hadn't meant to look, but it happened to catch my eye.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t016: But today, it still didn't feel like that kind of day.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t017: Just going outside felt like it would drain my strength.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t018: Even so, I thought it would be better than staying in my room, so I went out.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t020: Going out without a destination is harder than you might think.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t021: If you don't decide where to go, your feet choose the road they know best.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t022: A familiar road only shows you familiar scenery.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t023: Even so, today I was tired of familiar roads.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t024: I thought I might go somewhere just a little farther away.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t025: There was no reason. I just felt like something might change a little.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t026: I pulled out my bicycle and unlocked it.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t027: The saddle had grown hot in the sunlight.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t028: The heat soaking through my shorts made summer feel real.
chapter6.chapter6_start.t029: With no particular destination, I started pedaling.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t002: I felt like I had seen a notice on the bulletin board about the badminton club having practice matches.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t003: It wasn't as if I had any business there.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t004: My feet had simply turned that way.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t005: I parked my bicycle and went inside the entrance.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t006: The chilled air pricked my skin once.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t007: Coming in from the summer outside, the inside of a gym always felt like it held a different time.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t008: A sealed space untouched by the heat outside.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t009: There were almost no people in the viewing seats.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t010: Three or four club members' parents sat in folding chairs.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t011: Some held fans. Some pointed their phones toward the court.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t012: I leaned against the wall at the edge and looked over the courts.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t013: On one of them, Sakura was moving.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t016: She was somehow different from the Sakura I saw at school.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t017: In the classroom, she was always moving while reading the air around her.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t018: If someone laughed, she laughed. If someone was troubled, she moved first. She moved as if she were always watching the whole room.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t019: Here, she watched only what was inside the court.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t020: The parents outside it, the other club members, even me, did not exist.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t021: There was only the net, the shuttle, and her opponent.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t024: The club members around her called out.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t025: Sakura simply returned to her next position.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t026: No fist pump. No flashy celebration.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t027: Her transition was so fast it was almost quiet.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t028: (...I didn't know she had a face like that.)
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t029: I couldn't read the flow of the match,
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t030: but I could tell that each time Sakura moved, the air on the court was pulled along with her.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t031: She really changed when she came to places like this.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t033: maybe this was her true speed.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t034: If Sakura in the classroom was Sakura adjusted to ordinary speed,
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t035: then the way she moved on the court was probably her raw speed when the adjustment came off.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t038: For only an instant, at the edge of the court, she pulled up the towel around her neck and wiped her face.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t039: There was not a single piece of that movement meant to be shown to anyone.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t042: Outside the gym, parents had come to pick people up.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t043: Club members disappeared toward the parking lot, laughing.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t044: "Good work." "You were great today." "Where should we eat on the way home?"
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t045: Voices overlapped, and the lobby brightened a little.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t046: Sakura was nodding as the coach said something to her.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t047: Her profile was calm and looked the same as always.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t048: She answered with a smile, nodded again, and then the coach left.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t050: A voice came from the lobby.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t051: It was Hashimoto-senpai, a third-year and the badminton club captain.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t052: The first-years were moving better today.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t053: It made me think you really have been watching them properly.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t055: They still have a lot to work on, though.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t056: No, seriously, you're helping a lot.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t057: I'm already at my limit thinking about my own matches, so I can't keep track of all the little things with the juniors.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t058: I'm retiring in two months, so including what comes after that, can I leave things to you?
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t060: For just an instant, Sakura narrowed her eyes.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t062: I could tell Hashimoto-senpai meant, "You're doing things I can't do."
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t063: Whether that was gratitude, delegation, or an expectation taken for granted...
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t064: Sakura probably had no intention of asking from the start.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t065: Hashimoto-senpai's mother waved from near the exit.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t066: Oh, my ride's here. I'll head out first.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t067: Good work today. See you Monday.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t070: Sakura quietly bowed toward her retreating back.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t071: She wasn't doing it to show anyone.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t072: She did it because doing so was simply natural to her.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t074: Sakura was alone by the wall in the shade.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t075: She sat with her back against it.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t076: It wasn't the face of someone waiting for anyone.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t077: It was the face of someone who wasn't waiting.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t078: Her sports bag sat at her feet.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t079: Even when laughter drifted over from the parking lot,
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t081: It wasn't that she couldn't hear it.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t082: She could. She heard all of it, and still chose not to turn.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t083: If it were me, I would probably pretend I couldn't hear.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t084: Maybe Sakura truly hadn't heard it from the beginning.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t086: She heard it, and from the beginning, treated it as simply the way things were.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t087: Maybe she no longer felt anger or sadness over the fact that no one was coming to pick her up.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t088: If anything was there, it had settled into her body not as an emotion, but as a plain fact.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t089: That was the feeling in the way she sat.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t091: If I spoke to her, Sakura would probably smile like always.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t092: She would laugh and say, "Kirishima, you were here?"
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t093: and act as if nothing had happened.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_gym.t096: even knowing that, today I felt there was meaning in speaking to her.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t002: There was a beat of silence.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t005: But the smile came just a little late.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t006: It took her a little time
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t007: to overwrite the face from earlier, the face that had been waiting for no one, with a smile.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t008: She probably didn't notice it herself.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t009: I watched the match. You were amazing.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t010: Huh, you were watching? That's embarrassing.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t011: Did my movement look weird? I felt like I got a little sloppy in that last rally.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t012: It didn't look that way to me.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t014: Her expression turned a little serious.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t015: If the usual Sakura talked while laughing, this Sakura was talking without laughing.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t016: The difference seemed small, but it was large.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t017: Sakura moved away from the wall and stood.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t018: She gripped the strap of her sports bag with both hands.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t020: For an instant, Sakura's eyes turned toward the parking lot.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t021: Then they immediately turned back to me.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t024: After saying only that, Sakura smiled again.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t025: This time, it came properly fast.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t031: Not suspiciously, just... a little curiously.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t032: Kirishima, you're always watching, aren't you?
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t035: It was a strange way to say it.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t037: But being pointed out made something shift a little.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t039: A little. It catches my attention, I guess.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t040: Sometimes it feels like you're observing.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t043: Sakura said that and smiled a little.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t044: This time, the smile had no delay.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t045: It felt just a little closer to real.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t046: ...But when I'm being watched, I do get a little nervous.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t048: During the match, I didn't notice.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t049: After saying that, Sakura tilted her head slightly.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t050: ...Oh, right. So that's why I didn't notice.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t051: Inside the court, there isn't a wall at the edge like me.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t054: Did Sakura understand everything those words meant when she said them?
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t055: Did she say them knowingly, or did they slip out unconsciously?
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t056: Either way, the words carried a slightly different weight.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t057: Are you heading home too, Kirishima? I don't mind walking together partway.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t058: ...I'm going to wander a little first.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t062: I was saying that the whole time. I didn't notice.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t063: ...After matches, I might get like this.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t064: My feelings don't switch over in time.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t068: ...I'm stopping by the hospital for a bit too.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t070: My dad's been having tests done.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t071: I don't think it's anything major, but I thought I'd see his face before going home.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t073: See you, Yu. See you Monday.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t074: Sakura's back disappeared around the corner.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t075: No one had come to pick her up.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t076: But she walked on as if that were already natural.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t077: There is a way people walk when they are used to going home alone.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t078: Straight, but with just a little looseness in their body.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t079: Sakura's back had that kind of walk.
chapter6.chapter6_sakura_talk.t080: Getting used to something and not being hurt by it are probably different things.

chapter6.chapter6_kotoha_piano.t001: On the way back from the gym.
chapter6.chapter6_kotoha_piano.t002: Once I entered the shopping street, the summer heat changed a little.
chapter6.chapter6_kotoha_piano.t003: The arcade roof cut off the sunlight, and in its place all kinds of voices and smells had gathered.
chapter6.chapter6_kotoha_piano.t004: In the middle of an August day, the adults walked quickly.
chapter6.chapter6_kotoha_piano.t005: Sale signs. Shaved ice banners. The scent of water like after a goldfish-scooping stall had closed.
chapter6.chapter6_kotoha_piano.t006: Just because it was summer vacation didn't mean the adult world was on break.

bad_end.bad_end_mahiru_fragment.t015: No one had taken it home.
bad_end.bad_end_mahiru_fragment.t016: The teacher looked at it in silence.
bad_end.bad_end_mahiru_fragment.t017: I looked at it in silence too.
bad_end.bad_end_mahiru_fragment.t018: I didn't take that textbook either.

bad_end.bad_end_classroom.t002: I alone remained in the classroom after everyone else had gone.

bad_end.bad_end_classroom.t006: A place that had been so full of voices in the morning
bad_end.bad_end_classroom.t007: looked like a different building at night.
bad_end.bad_end_classroom.t008: Only I stood in the same place.

bad_end.bad_end_monologue.t001: It had been the same with Shin.
bad_end.bad_end_monologue.t002: It had been the same with my father.
bad_end.bad_end_monologue.t006: I couldn't pick up any of them.
bad_end.bad_end_monologue.t008: Even though this time, I should have been watching.
bad_end.bad_end_monologue.t009: Even so, my hand didn't reach.
bad_end.bad_end_monologue.t011: Those words fell into the empty classroom.
bad_end.bad_end_monologue.t012: I thought I had used a terrible phrase.
bad_end.bad_end_monologue.t014: Even though that night, I had hated it so much.
bad_end.bad_end_monologue.t015: A convenient, light phrase that saves nothing.
bad_end.bad_end_monologue.t016: A phrase that lets only the person saying it feel relieved,
bad_end.bad_end_monologue.t017: and leaves only the person hearing it silent.
bad_end.bad_end_monologue.t018: I wasn't saying it to anyone.
bad_end.bad_end_monologue.t020: I had only told it to myself.
bad_end.bad_end_ending.t001: Outside the window, the sky remained cloudy.
bad_end.bad_end_ending.t002: The stars still could not be seen.
bad_end.bad_end_ending.t003: Next time, will I be able to properly reach out?
bad_end.bad_end_ending.t004: Back when I quit track, I had thought the same thing.
bad_end.bad_end_ending.t010: Once again, I didn't make it in time.
bad_end.bad_end_ending.end_title: On a Morning Without Stars, I Was Alone Again

The juniors gathered their things, said goodbye, and left.
Sakura was cleaning up the court afterward.
Folding the net, checking the line tape.
One thing at a time, carefully.
A month had already passed since Hashimoto-senpai retired.
Until the next captain was officially decided, Sakura, as vice captain, was supposed to oversee practice.
But in reality, it was almost the same as running the whole club alone.
Sakura was used to the quiet after someone disappeared.
It looked like it had soaked into her body.
Before getting ready to leave, we stopped by the storage room.
Sakura was standing at the entrance.
Holding a racket, she looked into the back of the shelves.

route_sakura.sakura_ch10_outside.t004: The sounds of the gym were distant beyond one door.

route_sakura.sakura_ch10_outside.t014: But she did not move from the back of the storage room.
